checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: OtherAssetsNoncurrent only reported through 2011-09-30 (23,134.32); no goodwill/intangible line items provided in dataset

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?
Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: InventoryNet 2014-03-31 = 2,845,521.90 vs 2014-06-30 = 6,745,481.96 (137% quarter-over-quarter increase) while SalesRevenueGoodsNet grew only from 12,241,710.41 (Q1'14) to 16,181,851.14 (Q2'14), ~32%

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?
Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?
Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?
Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Revenues annual 2011 = 1,537,823.78 vs SalesRevenueNet annual 2011 = 50,091,900.36, and SalesRevenueServicesNet annual 2011 = 50,091,900.36 for the same fiscal year, filed in different accessions

Total Assets jumped from 48,518,766.30 (2013-09-30) to 112,136,940.88 (2013-12-31) with no itemized 'other current assets' or investment line provided to explain the ~54M gap versus AR/Inventory/Cash movements

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Potential premature or aggressive revenue recognition (e.g., extended credit terms, channel stuffing, or bill-and-hold arrangements) causing receivables to grow faster than sales and days-sales-outstanding to lengthen from ~90 to ~115 days in H1 2014, while the bad-debt allowance was not scaled proportionately, understating credit-risk reserves and overstating net receivables and reported revenue.

rationale_evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent 2014-06-30=20,565,196.82 vs 2013-12-31=9,082,048.73 (up ~126%) while quarterly revenue (SalesRevenueGoodsNet) rose only from 9,838,175.86 (Q4'13) to 16,181,851.14 (Q2'14) (~64%)
AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent grew slower (94,972.49 to 155,852.29) than AR (9,082,048.73 to 20,565,196.82), lowering the allowance-to-AR ratio from ~1.05% to ~0.76%

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Large non-operating/non-cash gains (e.g., fair-value remeasurement of warrants/derivatives, gain on debt extinguishment, or a bargain-purchase/business-combination gain) recorded in Q4 2013 appear to have driven net income to 50.7M for FY2013 (60.6M in Q4 alone) despite operating cash flow remaining negative (-16.16M) and a simultaneous unexplained ~54M jump in current assets and ~60M jump in equity, suggesting earnings quality issues or a one-time item inflating reported profitability without corresponding cash generation.

rationale_evidence.quote: Assets grew from 48,518,766.30 (2013-09-30) to 112,136,940.88 (2013-12-31); StockholdersEquity grew from 38,663,544.10 to 98,563,180.42 in the same quarter

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Inventory build materially outpacing revenue growth in early-mid 2014 could indicate overproduction/overstocking to support revenue targets or delayed recognition of obsolescence/write-downs, potentially overstating current assets and understating cost of goods sold.

rationale_evidence.quote: InventoryNet rose from 2,845,521.90 (2014-03-31) to 6,745,481.96 (2014-06-30), a 137% quarterly increase, while SalesRevenueGoodsNet rose only ~32% (12,241,710.41 to 16,181,851.14) over the same quarter

top_signals: NT 10-K late-filing notice preceding FY2013 10-K, indicating reporting/process strain (filing_chronology 2014-03-17 NT 10-K)
Massive divergence between FY2013 net income (50.7M, with 60.6M in Q4 alone) and negative operating cash flow (-16.16M)
Accounts receivable growing roughly twice as fast as revenue in H1 2014 with DSO extending from ~90 to ~115 days, while bad-debt allowance failed to scale proportionately
Unexplained ~54M jump in current assets and ~60M jump in equity in Q4 2013 not fully accounted for by disclosed AR/inventory/cash changes
Inconsistent/conflicting revenue tagging across concepts (Revenues vs SalesRevenueNet vs SalesRevenueServicesNet) for the same periods, reducing confidence in trend analysis
Inventory more than doubling quarter-over-quarter (Q1→Q2 2014) far outpacing revenue growth
